name: ruby-method-source
title: ruby-method-source
summary: Retrieve the sourcecode for a method as a Ruby string
description:
Retrieve the sourcecode for a method as a Ruby string
The `method_source` is a utility to return a method's sourcecode as a
Ruby string. Also returns `Proc` and `Lambda` sourcecode.
.
Method comments can also be extracted using the `comment` method.
.
It is written in pure Ruby (no C).
- Some Ruby 1.8 support now available.
- Support for MRI, RBX, JRuby, REE
.
`method_source` provides the `source` and `comment` methods to the
`Method` and `UnboundMethod` and `Proc` classes.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-method-source because Retrieve the sourcecode for a method as a Ruby string.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-method-source as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-method-source with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-method-source --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
